# schedule.pizza

> easiest way to find a time.

schedule.pizza is a small calendar scheduling service built around shareable
booking codes. A host shares a schedule.pizza link with a code. A human or agent
can use that capability to list availability, book an exact slot, or ask for a
time across several people.

## Docs

- [Docs](https://schedule.pizza/docs): Human and agent guide for availability,
  booking, group scheduling, recommendations, and calendar errors.
- [API index](https://schedule.pizza/api/v1): Machine-readable JSON index of the
  v1 endpoints, limits, copyable request examples, and typed error codes.
- [Group scheduling](https://schedule.pizza/group): Minimal human UI for finding
  a time across several schedule.pizza links.
- [Security policy](https://schedule.pizza/.well-known/security.txt): Security
  contact and reporting policy.

## Core API

- `GET /api/v1/availability?url=https%3A%2F%2Fschedule.pizza%2Falice%3Fcode%3Dmoon-tiger-seven`
- `POST /api/v1/book`
- `POST /api/v1/schedule`
- `POST /api/v1/recommend`
- `POST /api/v1/book-group`
- `GET /api/v1/account`
- `POST /api/v1/me/bootstrap`
- `POST /api/v1/me/booking-code`
- `GET /api/v1/account/bookings`
- `POST /api/v1/account/bookings/:bookingId/cancel`

All calendar times are UTC ISO 8601 strings ending in `Z`. Booking codes are
capabilities. Calls without a valid code should not expose availability. Google
Calendar event ids stay server-side. Recommendation calls return exact slots
first, then ranked alternatives only when no exact slot exists.

## Host agents

Signed-in host agents use the Better Auth session cookie. `GET /api/v1/account`
returns profile state and the newest generated `bookingUrl` when a code is
created or rotated. `GET /api/v1/account/bookings` returns upcoming confirmed
bookings with `kind` and a structured `cancel` object. Individual bookings can
be cancelled through the account cancellation endpoint. Group bookings are
visible to each host, but the group organizer cancels the shared event from
Google Calendar.
